EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving storm brought widespread strong and damaging winds to western and west-central Nevada on November 20.  There was also up to 3 inches of snowfall for portions of far western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Nevada Highway Patrol reported that, at 1218PST, near zero visibility caused a large accident on westbound Interstate 80 about 20 miles east of Fernley.  The accident involved 4 tractor trailer rigs, 3 pickups, and an SUV and caused one fatality along with 7 injuries.  Elsewhere, there were numerous trees and power lines down as well as major damage to one house in Fernley (due to fallen tree).||Here are some high wind gusts which contributed to the dust storm:  Pyramid Lake Fisheries with 60 mph at 1045PST, Silver Springs AWOS with 70 mph at 1315PST, and the Fallon ASOS with 59 mph at 1344PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving storm brought widespread strong and damaging winds to western and west-central Nevada on November 20.  There was also up to 3 inches of snowfall for portions of far western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There were an exceptionally numerous and widespread number of high wind reports with this storm.  Here is a list of some of the highest reported gusts: 68 mph in the Carson Valley near Minden at 0920PST, 83 mph in southeast Reno at 0920PST and at the NWS in Reno at 1318PST, and 71 mph at the Reno-Tahoe International Airport at 1323PST. ||The high winds caused damage (trees, fences, and power lines) and power outages throughout the area, including 2,780 people in Carson City.  In addition, there were 4 flight cancellations and around a dozen flight delays at the Reno-Tahoe International Airport from 1300PST to 1500PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms brought gusty winds and hail to western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter located 3 miles southeast of Fallon reported hail of 1-inch diameter.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A wildfire in Washoe Valley south of Reno was believed to have been caused by a lightning strike.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wildfire covering approximately 14 acres threatened homes in the Eastlake Boulevard and Douglas Boulevard area in Washoe Valley.  The Reno Gazette-Journal reported that the fire had burned to within 200 feet of at least one of the homes.  The fire was thought to have been started by a lightning strike.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter reported that a 20-ft tall 2-ft diameter tree was blown down onto a fence. Numerous small branches from other trees were also broken by the strong wind gusts.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms brought gusty winds to western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter reported wind gusts to 43 knots (50 mph) near Fallon Station. The strong wind produced blowing dust which reduced visibility to around 3 miles. A tree was blown over onto a power line which resulted in a loss of power for a few minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms brought gusty winds to western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 56 knot (64 mph) wind gust was recorded at the Lovelock airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms occurred across the eastern Sierra and far western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A dust devil was observed in a field west of Pyramid Highway at Calle de la Plata north of Reno.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms brought small hail and locally heavy rainfall to western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter reported three-quarter-inch hail between Sun Valley and Sparks.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms brought small hail and locally heavy rainfall to western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Churchill County Sheriff's Office reported flash flooding near Middlegate Station.  The flash flooding was affecting Nevada Highway 722 and U.S. Highway 50 between Eastgate and Middlegate.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving storm brought widespread strong and damaging winds to western and west-central Nevada on November 20.  There was also up to 3 inches of snowfall for portions of far western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: According to the Mineral County Independent News in Hawthorne, there was a power outage from 1500PST to 2100PST as well as some windows broken in Hawthorne.  A large part of a roof in Hawthorne was blown off. ||Here are some high wind gusts from the region: Schurz with 64 mph at 1133PST, Yerington AWOS with 59 at 1315PST, Brawley Peak RAWS (8080 ft elevation) with 76 mph at 1447PST, and Hawthorne AWOS with 72 mph at 1458PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very cold and intense upper low dropped from British Columbia into western Oregon on the 6th and into northern California on the 7th.  This brought heavy snow to the Sierra and western Nevada along with strong winds for the higher terrain of the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An observer in Yerington reported 4.5 to 5 inches of snowfall on the 7th, with schools being closed on the 8th.  A HADS observation 15 to 20 miles south of Hawthorne had an estimated 4-6 inches of snowfall.

CZ_NAME: LINCOLN COUNTY EXCEPT THE SHEEP RANGE
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another Pacific low pressure system brought breezy to windy conditions to much of the Mojave Desert.  One high wind gust was reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 12 miles SW of Elgin.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system and associated cold front brought locally high winds to the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 1 mile S of Mercury.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system and associated cold front brought locally high winds to the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 6 miles SSE of Hayford Peak.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture fueled an outbreak of thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert.  Several storms produced high winds and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water, rocks and debris washed over roads on Mount Charleston. A rain gage measured 1.14 inches of rain in one hour.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system and associated cold front brought locally high winds to the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: These gusts occurred 1 mile ESE of Red Rock Canyon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture fueled an outbreak of thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert.  Several storms produced high winds and flash flooding.
Monsoon moisture fueled an outbreak of thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert.  Several storms produced high winds and flash flooding.
Strong heating helped fuel a dust devil in Henderson.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A dust devil damaged approximately 12 homes in the Mountain View Mobile Home Park.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Three people died of heat related causes in the Las Vegas Valley.  Temperatures had been at or above 80 degrees 24 hours a day since June 27th, and afternoon highs had been at or above 103 degrees all but one day since June 27th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One man died in Las Vegas of heat related causes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Three people died of heat related causes in the Las Vegas Valley.  Temperatures had been at or above 80 degrees 24 hours a day since June 27th, and afternoon highs had been at or above 103 degrees all but one day since June 27th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One man died in Las Vegas of heat related causes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably strong area of low pressure brought strong southwest winds to the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 1 mile S of Mercury.  Another station 3 miles SSW of Mercury recorded a gust of 58 knots at approximately the same time.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level disturbance and monsoon moisture combined to trigger an outbreak of thunderstorms across the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Henderson Airport AWOS measured a 58 mph wind gust at 527 PM.  13 minutes later, the same thunderstorm blew down three 15-foot tall mesquite trees at the intersection of Pecos and Pebble.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level disturbance and monsoon moisture combined to trigger an outbreak of thunderstorms across the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Nipton Road (NV Highway 164) was closed due to flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Gusty southwest winds developed across the Mojave Desert ahead of a weak trough of low pressure.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 1 mile S of Mercury.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system and associated cold front brought high winds to the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 1 mile S of Mercury.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system and associated cold front brought high winds to the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 3 miles SSW of Mercury.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several 8-10 foot trees were blown down, and a large sign was destroyed in the extreme southwest corner of the Las Vegas Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system and associated cold front brought high winds to the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 33 miles E of Beatty.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought gusty southwest winds ahead of it, and gusty north winds behind it.  Isolated downslope winds were also seen ahead of the front.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 4 miles NW of Pahrump.

CZ_NAME: LINCOLN COUNTY EXCEPT THE SHEEP RANGE
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought gusty southwest winds ahead of it, and gusty north winds behind it.  Isolated downslope winds were also seen ahead of the front.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 12 miles SW of Elgin.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very cold and intense upper low dropped from British Columbia into western Oregon on the 6th and into northern California on the 7th.  This brought heavy snow to the Sierra and western Nevada along with strong winds for the higher terrain of the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Big Meadow SNOTEL (4 miles S of Mogul at 8300 ft) had an estimated 18 inches of snowfall.  Other snowfall reports include: Mt Rose Ski Area with 23 inches, the Dagget Pass and Glenbrook fire stations with 17 inches apiece.  In addition to heavy snowfall, winds were gusting 30-45 mph around the onset of the winter storm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very cold and intense upper low dropped from British Columbia into western Oregon on the 6th and into northern California on the 7th.  This brought heavy snow to the Sierra and western Nevada along with strong winds for the higher terrain of the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread totals of 6-10 inches were reported in the area. Here are some of the isolated higher totals: Carson City with 14-18 inches and Dayton with 13 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very cold and intense upper low dropped from British Columbia into western Oregon on the 6th and into northern California on the 7th.  This brought heavy snow to the Sierra and western Nevada along with strong winds for the higher terrain of the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A widespread 6-9 inches of snowfall fell generally south and west of Lovelock. Some specific totals include: 10-12 inches in Stagecoach, 9 inches in Fallon, and 6 inches in Fernley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought heavy snow on the order of 6 to 10 inches across portions of White Pine county and the Ruby Mountains and East Humboldt Range.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall reports received from White Pine county included 6 inches at Ely, Ruth, and Great Basin National Park.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A major late season storm dumped up to 2 feet of snow over portions northern and central Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall amounts included 8 inches at the Elko Weather Forecast Office and 20 inches in Spring Creek and Lamoille.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A major late season storm dumped up to 2 feet of snow over portions northern and central Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Eureka received 26 inches of new snow and Austin 15 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving cold front brought gusty winds to western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reported wind gust:||Galena RAWS, 60 kts (69 mph).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving cold front brought gusty winds to western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gust reports:|Galena RAWS, 60 kts (69 mph); |4 miles east of Verdi, 54 kts (62 mph); and |NWS Forecast Office in north Reno, 52 kts (60 mph).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably cold storm system produced high winds, thunderstorms, and isolated high elevation heavy snow in southern Nevada and southeast California.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Seven power poles were knocked down 13 miles N of Amargosa Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A prolonged episode of gusty southwest winds produced isolated high wind reports in southeast California, southern Nevada, and northwest Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 1 mile ESE of Red Rock Canyon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved across the area overnight on March 8th-9th.  Up to 6 inches of snow fell over portions of the northern Sierra and western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Storm-total snowfall reports: |Spanish Springs area of northeast Reno, 6.0 inches; and |Palomino Valley northeast of Reno, 6.0 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful winter storm moved through the northern and central Sierra Nevada and western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Storm-total snowfall amounts: |Diamond Peak ski area, 24 inches; |Mt. Rose ski area, 17 inches; and |Heavenly Ski Resort, 10 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong winds preceded a cold front as it approached the northern Sierra and western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 56 kt (64 mph) wind gust was recorded at the Dead Camel RAWS.  In the area around Fallon a fence was reported knocked down by the wind, small to medium sized branches were knocked off trees, and power outages were reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving low pressure system brought snowfall to western Nevada in early April.  Snowfall totals were less than 6 inches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Storm-total snowfall amounts:||Stead, 4 inches; |Reno/Tahoe Intl. Airport (elev. 4410 ft), 3 inches; and |3 miles NW of Carson City (elev. 5240 ft), 3 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure system brought snow and gusty winds to the eastern Sierra and western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A total of 3.3 inches of snowfall was recorded by the Sheldon SNOTEL (elev. 5860 ft).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: After a couple of days with near record high temperatures, mid level moisture advected in from the southeast, producing an inverted-v temperature and moisture profile over the Mojave Desert.  One cluster of light showers and thunderstorms produced locally high winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 33 miles E of Beatty.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A late-season storm brought strong winds to western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Nevada Dept. of Transportation (NDOT) weather sensor in Washoe Valley recorded a wind gust of 69 mph. Additional wind gusts of 60 mph or greater were recorded at the site from 340 pm PDT through 440 pm PDT.  The Galena RAWS recorded a wind gust of 51 knots (59 mph).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A late-season storm brought strong winds to western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Galena RAWS recorded a wind gust of 62 knots (71 mph). Wind gusts of 50 knots or greater were recorded from 700 pm PDT on May 4th through 400 am PDT May 5th.  The Nevada Dept. of Transportation (NDOT) weather sensor in Washoe Valley recorded a wind gust of 56 knots (65 mph). Wind gusts of 50 knots or greater were recorded from 1239 am through 149 am PDT on May 5th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms occurred across the eastern Sierra and western Nevada during the afternoon and evening of May 22nd.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter reported 1-inch diameter hail 3 miles SSW of Gardnerville.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Two EF0 tornadoes were reported in the vicinity of Wild Horse reservoir. No damage was reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A Highway Patrol Officer observed a tornado approximately 3 miles north of Wild Horse. No damage was observed or reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved through northeastern California and western Nevada on the 29th.  Strong winds ahead of the front caused damage across the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were recorded across the area.  The Reno Gazette-Journal reported that the strong winds caused a semi-trailer to blow over on U. S. Highway 395 near Stead.  An off-duty NWS employee reported a portion of a fence blown down in the Spanish Springs area northeast of Reno.  The Nevada DOT sensor at Five Mile Flat near Virginia City recorded a wind gust of 69 mph at 6:45 am PDT.  Also, the Galena RAWS recorded a wind gust of 66 mph at 11:03 am PDT.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved through northeastern California and western Nevada on the 29th.  Strong winds ahead of the front caused damage across the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Nevada Highway Patrol reported that the westbound lane of Interstate 80 was closed about 20 miles east of Fernley due to poor visibility caused by blowing dust.  Two tractor-trailer rigs ran off the road when the visibility was near zero.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably strong area of low pressure brought strong southwest winds to the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One mobile home was moved off its foundation, and the roof was blown off another.  Based on the description of the event, a dust devil is suspected.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front followed by lake effect snow brought 4 to 10 inches of snowfall east and southeast of Lake Tahoe from the evening of the 3rd to the morning of the 4th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A narrow lake effect band brought 5 to 10 inches of snowfall to non-paved areas just south of Gardnerville.  There was little apparent travel impact as there were no road problems noted per web cams and the lack of chain or snow tire requirements through the event.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front followed by lake effect snow brought 4 to 10 inches of snowfall east and southeast of Lake Tahoe from the evening of the 3rd to the morning of the 4th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Four inches of snowfall were reported in Stateline.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front followed by lake effect snow brought 4 to 10 inches of snowfall east and southeast of Lake Tahoe from the evening of the 3rd to the morning of the 4th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A narrow lake effect snow band caused snowfall amounts of 4 to 8 inches on non-paved surfaces in the Smith Valley southwest of Yerington. However, there were few road impacts noted as low temperatures were in the 30s and road surfaces were relatively warm.||Some tree limbs, still laden with leaves, broke and fell on power lines due to the weight of the heavy, wet snow. Around 100 customers were without power in the Smith Valley on the 4th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Remnants of a typhoon enhanced very moist flow off the Pacific Ocean and brought strong winds and unusually heavy to record-setting rainfall to the region.  An average of 1.5 to 2.5 inches of rainfall fell immediately east of the Sierra with 3 to 5 inches (locally as high as 6-7 inches) in the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Minor power outages were reported with these strong winds.  There were also isolated high wind gusts, mainly in typically wind-prone areas.  The isolated high wind reports include: Galena RAWS with 79 mph, Washoe Valley NDOT with 75 mph, Five Mile Flat NDOT with 71 mph, and the Reno ASOS with a very brief (less than 30 seconds) gust to 71 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving storm brought widespread strong and damaging winds to western and west-central Nevada on November 20.  There was also up to 3 inches of snowfall for portions of far western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Here are some snowfall amounts from November 20: Mt. Rose Ski Area with 8-10 inches, Big Meadow SNOTEL with an estimated 6-9 inches, and the Glenbrook CO-OP observer with 4 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving storm brought widespread strong and damaging winds to western and west-central Nevada on November 20.  There was also up to 3 inches of snowfall for portions of far western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds on Lake Tahoe were sustained at 40-50 mph.  There were no other sites which reached high wind criteria, however Kahle Drive and Glenbrook Canyon NDOT sensors recorded 45 and 47 mph gusts, respectively (gusts that high are notable given that these observations were in at least partially-sheltered locations).  Little Valley RAWS recorded a gust to 55 mph at 0838PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast-moving storm brought widespread strong and damaging winds to western and west-central Nevada on November 20.  There was also up to 3 inches of snowfall for portions of far western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There were numerous high wind gust reports with this system.  Catnip Mt. had 60 mph at 1144PST, Buffalo Creek recorded 62 mph at 1159PST, Juniper Springs had 59 mph at 1240PST, and Gerlach reported 61 mph at 1314PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A system moving south through the Central Valley of California brought breezy conditions and up to 2 inches of snowfall (above 4500 feet elevation) to portions of western Nevada.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread breezy conditions with gusts 30 to 40 mph were reported with this system. An isolated high wind gust was reported at the Galena RAWS (wind-prone sensor), which had a gust to 67 mph between 0900PST and 1000PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A vigorous cold front produced strong down slope winds along the lee side of the Santa Rosa Range and the Ruby Mountains/East Humboldt Range.
A vigorous cold front produced strong down slope winds along the lee side of the Santa Rosa Range and the Ruby Mountains/East Humboldt Range.

Remnants of a typhoon enhanced very moist flow off the Pacific Ocean and brought strong winds and unusually heavy to record-setting rainfall to the region.  An average of 1.5 to 2.5 inches of rainfall fell immediately east of the Sierra with 3 to 5 inches (locally as high as 6-7 inches) in the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Urban street flooding was reported at 1830PST in the area of Double Diamond Pkwy, South Meadows Pkwy, and Double R Blvd.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Remnants of a typhoon enhanced very moist flow off the Pacific Ocean and brought strong winds and unusually heavy to record-setting rainfall to the region.  An average of 1.5 to 2.5 inches of rainfall fell immediately east of the Sierra with 3 to 5 inches (locally as high as 6-7 inches) in the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Lyon County Emergency Manager reported street flooding in Dayton at 1745PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Remnants of a typhoon enhanced very moist flow off the Pacific Ocean and brought strong winds and unusually heavy to record-setting rainfall to the region.  An average of 1.5 to 2.5 inches of rainfall fell immediately east of the Sierra with 3 to 5 inches (locally as high as 6-7 inches) in the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Lyon County Emergency Manager reported street flooding in Fernley at 1745PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Remnants of a typhoon enhanced very moist flow off the Pacific Ocean and brought strong winds and unusually heavy to record-setting rainfall to the region.  An average of 1.5 to 2.5 inches of rainfall fell immediately east of the Sierra with 3 to 5 inches (locally as high as 6-7 inches) in the Sierra.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall caused a rock slide southeast of Yerington. A spotter reported a rock slide with boulders on the road in Wilson Canyon at 1830PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong sunshine and temperatures 10-15 degrees above average helped spin up a dust devil which caused damage in Boulder City.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A strong dust devil moved through the Gingerwood Mobile Home Park on the edge of Boulder City, damaging several mobile homes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: After a couple of days with near record high temperatures, mid level moisture advected in from the southeast, producing an inverted-v temperature and moisture profile over the Mojave Desert.  One cluster of light showers and thunderstorms produced locally high winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred at the Desert Rock Airport (KDRA).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Two EF0 tornadoes were reported in the vicinity of Wild Horse reservoir. No damage was reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A rope-like tornado was observed to touch down for approximately 45 to 90 seconds. The weather spotter was not able to take a picture while the tornado was on the ground, but got a picture after it had lifted about half way up to the cloud base. The Nevada Highway patrol did an aerial survey of the area afterward but found no evidence of damage.
